audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + bovendien + clause]] and [[grammar:zes, zeven, acht, negen, tien]]. Use [[grammar:bovendien]] for in addition or moreover. Learn six through ten and combine them with ordinary plural nouns.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + bovendien + clause]]: [[grammar:Bovendien]] is an additive connective adverb and triggers V2 when placed first.; [[grammar:zes, zeven, acht, negen, tien]]: Cardinals 6-10 precede plural count nouns and do not require a classifier.

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + bovendien + clause

Use [[grammar:bovendien]] for in addition or moreover. Example: [[grammar:Bovendien komen er vier nieuwe bestellingen binnen.]]

#### zes, zeven, acht, negen, tien

Learn six through ten and combine them with ordinary plural nouns. Example: [[grammar:Lotte zet negen kopjes op de ontbijttafel.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + bovendien + clause

[[grammar:Bovendien]] is an additive connective adverb and triggers V2 when placed first. Literal example: [[grammar:Bovendien komen er vier nieuwe bestellingen binnen.]]

#### zes, zeven, acht, negen, tien

Cardinals 6-10 precede plural count nouns and do not require a classifier. Literal example: [[grammar:Lotte zet negen kopjes op de ontbijttafel.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Bovendien]] adds information and can begin a V2 main clause. Numbers six through ten use ordinary plural nouns.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Bovendien]] adds information and can begin a V2 main clause. Numbers six through ten use ordinary plural nouns. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Milan leest zes bestellingen bij de kassa.]] — Milan reads six orders at the register.
- [[grammar:Bovendien komen er vier nieuwe bestellingen binnen.]] — In addition, four new orders come in.
- [[grammar:Samen zijn dat tien ontbijten.]] — Together that makes ten breakfasts.
- [[grammar:Lotte zet negen kopjes op de ontbijttafel.]] — Lotte puts nine cups on the breakfast table.
- [[grammar:Pieter brengt zeven borden uit de keuken.]] — Pieter brings seven plates from the kitchen.
- [[grammar:Milan telt acht croissants en twee broodjes.]] — Milan counts eight croissants and two rolls.
- [[grammar:Mark helpt, omdat de bestelling groot is.]] — Mark helps because the order is large.
- [[grammar:Bovendien vult hij zes glazen met water.]] — In addition, he fills six glasses with water.
- [[grammar:Lotte controleert alle tien bestellingen.]] — Lotte checks all ten orders.
- [[grammar:Om negen uur staat het ontbijt klaar.]] — At nine o’clock breakfast is ready.
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Milan leest zes bestellingen bij de kassa.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Bovendien komen er vier nieuwe bestellingen binnen.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Samen zijn dat tien ontbijten.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 3.
- [[grammar:Lotte zet negen kopjes op de ontbijttafel.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 1 > sentence 4.
- [[grammar:Pieter brengt zeven borden uit de keuken.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Milan telt acht croissants en twee broodjes.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Mark helpt, omdat de bestelling groot is.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 3.
- [[grammar:Bovendien vult hij zes glazen met water.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Lotte controleert alle tien bestellingen.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Om negen uur staat het ontbijt klaar.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 3.
